parameters.errorMessage: Missing required field 'email' on incoming candidate.

parameters.message: =Thanks for your interest — here's a quick-start guide.

parameters.text: =✅ Shortlist candidates with Google Sheets and Microsoft Teams at scale — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Candidate Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Role, Stage). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.email }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Score the Lead · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Score the Lead

Custom JavaScript: Score each candidate 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Assign Round-Robin Owner

### Assign Round-Robin Owner

Custom JavaScript: Round-robin owner assignment

Next: Route by Heat

### Route by Heat

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: hot, warm + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping · ②→ Warm → Drip via Microsoft Teams · ③→ Cold → Park in Nurture List

### Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping

Upserts a row into 'Candidate Tracker', matched on `candidateId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Notify Assigned Owner · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping

parameters.content:
### Notify Assigned Owner

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Warm → Drip via Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Warm → Drip via Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Cold → Park in Nurture List

Appends a new row to 'Candidate Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Cold → Park in Nurture List

parameters.content:
### Combine Lead Paths

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Candidate Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#hr`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#hr`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Shortlist candidates with Google Sheets and Microsoft Teams at scale

**Pattern:** Recruitment Pipeline · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Shortlist candidates with Google Sheets and Microsoft Teams at scale. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual onboarding is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Candidate Form Submission → Required Fields Present? → Score the Lead → Assign Round-Robin Owner → Route by Heat → Hot → Google Sheets + Instant Ping → Notify Assigned Owner → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to recruitment pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating onboarding in human resources
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Round-robin owner assignment happens right after scoring, before routing — every lead gets an accountable owner regardless of tier, so even a 'cold' lead parked in the nurture list is someone's responsibility later, not an anonymous row in a spreadsheet.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Change the hot/warm threshold values and watch the tier distribution shift.
2. Add a 4th owner to the round-robin pool.
3. Add a follow-up wait+check to the warm-drip branch, similar to the drip-ladder spine.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your onboarding process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
